destinations.evidence: The verifier reads AsyncJobTracker job data from parent state and matches each SPC call against the job precompile address, commit block, and input hash.
Missing executor keys, short proofs, signature errors, and invalid signatures are consensus rejections.

equivalence_argument: Every fulfilled HTTP/LLM SPC call is tied back to parent-state AsyncJobTracker and TEE registry data before shared signature verification; all listed malformed or missing-data paths return BlockVerificationError.
notes: Mapped to the port block verifier.
rationale: The merge moves executor key reads to the unified registry reader and the verifier documents parent-state contract data as consensus-critical; the implementation extracts SPC calls, reads jobs and executor keys from parent state, checks job block numbers, and hard-fails invalid signatures. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: During block verification, every HTTP/LLM SPC call in fulfilled async transactions must be verified against AsyncJobTracker job data and ExecutorRegistry public keys read from the parent block state, and missing jobs, missing executors, block-number mismatch, short proofs, or invalid signatures must reject the block.
suggested_falsification_tests: Build a block with a fulfilled async transaction containing an HTTP SPC call and vary parent-state AsyncJobTracker/ExecutorRegistry data: missing job, missing executor key, mismatched job commit block, proof shorter than the signature length, and bad signature should each cause verify_block to return an SPC verification error.
summary: SPC verification uses parent-state job and executor data and rejects missing jobs, bad block/input matches, short proofs, and invalid signatures.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Run verify_block with missing job, missing executor, mismatched commit block, short proof, bad signature, and valid signature variants; assert only the valid block passes.
title: SPC verification uses parent-state contract data
